name: MC Kombucha

name: Motherlands
classification_notes: Drawn from lettering found on World War II-era maps, placing it firmly in the display category as a historically-informed revival with a specific archival source rather than a text or body-copy application.
summary: A single-weight display face drawn from lettering found on World War II-era maps, recreated after the designer found no existing digital version.
description: Inspired by cartographic lettering encountered during library research into Second World War materials, this face fills a genuine gap where no digital revival previously existed. It ships as a single Regular weight and covers 74 languages. Best suited to display and headline use where a period map aesthetic is wanted.
foundry_blurb: The designer describes a personal fascination with maps and a discovery of distinctive letterforms on World War II-era cartography during library research. Finding no existing digital replication, they drew the typeface themselves, naming it Motherlands and building in support for 74 languages.
